title: Neighbourhood Characterization of Discontinuity
statement: Let $A \subseteq \mathbb{R}$, let $f \colon A \to \mathbb{R}$, and let $c \in A$. The function $f$ is discontinuous at $c$ if and only if there exists a neighborhood $V$ of $f(c)$ such that for every neighborhood $U$ of $c$ relative to $A$ there exists $x \in U \cap A$ with $f(x) \notin V$.
